title: Deaths Accounted For (DAF)
description: A lightweight mod focused on tracking kills, deaths, and enforcing a configurable death limit for competitive or hardcore servers.
body:
Tired of your friends claiming they’re the “_best PvPer on the server_” with nothing to back it up? Or maybe you just want to know who really gets wrecked in fair fights (spoiler: still probably you).

**Deaths Accounted For** keeps track of every glorious victory and every not-so-glorious defeat — but only when another player is involved. Creepers don’t count here. No more excuses, no more “_lag got me_” — the stats don’t lie.

Whether you’re running a hardcore challenge, a PvP tournament, or just want to roast your friends with cold, hard numbers, this mod’s got you covered.

 

### **✨ Features**
- **Kills & Deaths Tracking** – Records only player-caused kills and deaths, complete with player names and timestamps.
- **Player Stats Command** – Run /stats to check your own stats. Admins can peek at everyone else’s, but normal players only see their own.
- **Leaderboard Command** – Shows the top players ranked by kills only. Simple, clean, and ruthless.
- **Configurable Death Limit** – Want hardcore mode with a twist? Set a death cap — when players reach it, they’re automatically banned. (Don’t worry, you can tweak or disable it in the config.)
- **Reloadable Config and Stats** – Change settings without restarting the server. Because who has time for restarts?
- **Death and Ban Logs** - Keep track of every kill and bans with timestamps available in the Log file.



### **🎮 Server Use Cases**
- **PvP Arenas** – Track who’s truly the champion.
- **Hardcore SMPs** – Add accountability and spice with a configurable death cap.
- **Events & Tournaments** – Make competitions fair with a visible leaderboard.
- **Friendly Chaos** – Or just keep it around to remind your friends who the real “PvP god” is.

  

If you have any questions, sugestions or just wanna have a good time. feel free to join my [Mike's Land](https://discord.gg/S5EESWTtMe)
